questionHtml:
What assumption is made when using the Hardy-Weinberg equation for calculating changes in allele frequencies in a population?


A.  The population size is large.

B.  Natural selection is taking place.

C.  Mutations are occurring in the population.

D.  There is variation among the phenotypes of the population.

meta.title: What assumption is made when using the Hardy-Weinberg equation for calculating changes in allele frequencies in a population? A. The population size is large. B. Natural selection
breadcrumbLabels: D4.1.13. Hardy–Weinberg equation and calculations of allele or genotype frequencies
